name: Viaducto del Río Guadiana y Playa fluvial de Entrerrios — circular desde Villanueva de la Serena
region_intro: Hiking around Villanueva De La Serena offers diverse trails through the Extremaduran landscape. The region features a mix of agricultural plains, river systems like the Guadiana, and historical sites such as Magacela Castle. Hikers can explore areas with ancient dolmens, canals, and natural lagoons, providing varied terrain for outdoor activities. Best hiking trails around Villanueva De La Serena The most popular hiking route is Dolmen de Magacela — circular por la Laguna de los Santos, an easy 2.6 miles (4.2 km) trail that takes 1 hour 14 minutes to complete. This circular path explores the area around the Laguna de los Santos. Another top favourite among local hikers is Magacela Castle and Mozarabic Way Loop, a moderate 10.2 miles (16.5 km) path. This route combines historical exploration with views of the surrounding countryside. Local hikers also love the Viaducto del Río Guadiana y Playa fluvial de Entrerrios — circular desde Villanueva de la Serena, a 17.4 miles (28.1 km) trail leading through river landscapes and past a fluvial beach, often completed in about 7 hours 9 minutes. Hiking around Villanueva De La Serena is defined by river valleys, historical sites, and agricultural plains. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and more challenging routes suitable for various ability levels. The routes in Villanueva De La Serena are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.9 stars from over 9 reviews. More than 130 hikers have used komoot to explore Villanueva De La Serena's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Villanueva De La Serena?
A: There are over 40 hiking routes recorded around Villanueva De La Serena on komoot, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels and preferences.

Q: Are there any easy circular hiking routes suitable for a relaxed walk?
A: Yes, for an easy circular walk, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1293570180/dolmen-de-magacela-circular-por-la-laguna-de-los-santos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dolmen de Magacela — circular por la Laguna de los Santos&lt;/a&gt;. This 2.6-mile (4.2 km) trail takes about an hour and explores the area around the Laguna de los Santos, offering a pleasant and accessible experience.

Q: What historical sites or landmarks can I see while hiking in the region?
A: Many trails in Villanueva De La Serena pass by significant historical sites. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1292088322/magacela-castle-and-mozarabic-way-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Magacela Castle and Mozarabic Way Loop&lt;/a&gt; offers views of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3035140" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Magacela Castle&lt;/a&gt;. You can also find ancient structures like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2940960" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dolmen de Magacela&lt;/a&gt; on some routes.

Q: Are there any trails that feature water or river landscapes?
A: Absolutely. The region is characterized by river systems like the Guadiana. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1293596923/viaducto-del-rio-guadiana-y-playa-fluvial-de-entrerrios-circular-desde-villanueva-de-la-serena" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viaducto del Río Guadiana y Playa fluvial de Entrerrios — circular desde Villanueva de la Serena&lt;/a&gt; is a longer trail that leads through river landscapes and past a fluvial beach, offering beautiful waterside views.

Q: What is the best season for hiking in Villanueva De La Serena?
A: The Extremaduran climate means spring and autumn are generally the most pleasant seasons for hiking, with milder temperatures. Summers can be very hot, making early morning or late afternoon hikes preferable, while winters are generally mild but can be damp.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options in Villanueva De La Serena?
A: Many of the easy-grade trails are suitable for families. Routes that are shorter and have minimal elevation gain, such as those exploring agricultural plains or around natural lagoons, are often ideal for families with children. Always check the specific route details for suitability.

Q: Can I find trails with good parking access?
A: Most trailheads in the Villanueva De La Serena area, especially those starting from towns or popular attractions, typically offer parking facilities. It's always recommended to check the starting point details of your chosen komoot tour for specific parking information.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Villanueva De La Serena?
A: The trails in Villanueva De La Serena are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 3.9 stars from over 9 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse Extremaduran landscape, which includes agricultural plains, river systems, and historical sites.

Q: Are there any trails that pass by lakes or offer opportunities for wild swimming?
A: While specific wild swimming spots directly on trails are not always marked, the region features natural lagoons and lakes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5667110" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Playa de Orellana&lt;/a&gt; is a notable beach area on a lake, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5462406" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de las Casas del Montecillo&lt;/a&gt; is another natural lake nearby that might offer scenic views or opportunities for relaxation.

Q: Are there any moderate trails for hikers looking for a bit more challenge?
A: Yes, for a moderate challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1293551962/ascent-to-el-palmeral-via-the-vereda-de-don-venito-to-vilanueva-de-la-serena-circular-from-don-benito" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent to El Palmeral via the Vereda de Don Venito to Vilanueva de la Serena — circular from Don Benito&lt;/a&gt; is a good option. This 5-mile (8 km) route offers a bit more elevation and takes around 2.5 hours to complete, providing a rewarding experience.

Q: Can I access hiking trails using public transport in Villanueva De La Serena?
A: While some trails might be accessible from urban centers like Villanueva De La Serena or Don Benito, public transport options directly to remote trailheads can be limited. It's advisable to check local bus schedules or consider driving to the starting points of your chosen routes.

Q: Are there any trails that follow canals or waterways?
A: Yes, the region's agricultural plains often feature canals. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1293545291/don-benito-by-the-canal-del-zujar-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Don Benito by the Canal del Zújar — circular&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 4-mile (6.5 km) route that follows the Canal del Zújar, offering pleasant views along the waterway.
tips: Bridge that allows us to cross to the other side of the Río Guadiana and that is pedestrian. It's worth getting there! It is part of the greenway that goes to Villanueva de la Serena.

Entrerríos Beach is a charming place located on the right bank of the Zújar River, near Villanueva de la Serena, in Extremadura. Here you can enjoy a natural environment with a grass area, shade provided by trees, a beach bar, picnic area and a children's playground. It is perfect for a day of bathing and recreation in good company.
Definitely worth a visit! 😊
